name: Breckland Farmers Wildlife Network
organisationDescription: a farmer-led group collaborating to create a landscape-scale habitat network across the 400 square miles of the Norfolk & Suffolk Brecks. This is a very important area for food production but also home to a huge range of species, many of which are found nowhere else in the UK.

name: Climate Change Committee
organisationDescription: The Climate Change Committee was established under the Climate Change Act 2008 and advises the UK and devolved governments on reducing emissions and adapting to the impacts of climate change.

name: Crown Point Estate
organisationDescription: Crown Point Estate comprises a patchwork of nearly 8 square miles of farmland on the edge of Norwich beyond Trowse.  Owned and run by the Colman family since 1872, the estate is also home to Whitlingham Country Park, one of the city's best-loved nature and leisure spots.

name: JNCC
organisationDescription: We are the Joint Nature Conservation Committee (JNCC), the only statutory nature advisor to all four countries of the UK. We provide robust scientific evidence and advice to help decision makers turn science into action for nature to guide the UK on a sustainable path. We work across land and sea with partners throughout the UK, the UK Overseas Territories, the Crown Dependencies and around the world.

name: Natural England
organisationDescription: We were established by an Act of Parliament in 2006. Our purpose is to help conserve, enhance and manage the natural environment for the benefit of present and future generations, thereby contributing to sustainable development.

name: Nature Scotland
organisationDescription: We are Scotland's nature agency. We work to improve our natural environment in Scotland and inspire everyone to care more about it. So that all nature in Scotland - our biodiversity, geodiversity and the natural elements of our landscapes and seascapes - is maintained, enhanced and brings us benefits.

name: Westcountry Rivers Trust
organisationDescription: What we do is underpinned by science, evidence and experience and a commitment to the resilience of the region’s freshwater resources. Whether we are working with farming or fishing communities, improving river habitats, mapping landscapes or monitoring streams, it is all to bring our rivers to life and keep them healthy for wildlife and people, both now and in the future.

name: University of East Anglia
organisationDescription: Our stunning and vibrant Norwich campus is home to a global community pursuing personal and professional success. At UEA we come together from different fields to learn, innovate, collaborate and help create a better future.

name: British Trust for Ornithology
organisationDescription: We are a Registered Charity, working to secure the future for birds and nature, using our science, monitoring and data to inform good environmental decisions, inspiring others with the wonder of birds and empowering them to work with us. Our priority is to make a positive impact for birds and people, using our strong and objective science in partnership with others. We strive to work sustainably and to welcome everyone.

name: University of Plymouth
organisationDescription: The University of Plymouth takes a solutions-oriented, systems-thinking approach to solving complex global challenges, addressing critical issues through the lenses of environment, society, economy and place to create strong, sustainable research impact beyond academia. Aligning globally to the UN Sustainable Development Goals, our work aims to drive sustainability both on land and in the marine environment, improve human health and care, and support communities. Our research also drives innovation on our doorstep,and we contribute to the economic growth and development of the South West region.

name: University of Leeds
organisationDescription: Established in 1904, the University of Leeds is one of the largest universities in the UK. We're part of the Russell Group of research-intensive universities and are renowned globally for the quality of our research

name: UKCEH
organisationDescription: For over 60 years, UKCEH and its predecessor institutes have been at the forefront of environmental monitoring and modelling. The long-term environmental data hosted by UKCEH inform policymaking, enable new discoveries, and drive research and innovation.

name: UKRI-STFC
organisationDescription: STFC's mission is to deliver world-leading national and international research and innovation capabilities and, through those, discover the secrets of the Universe.

name: RSPB
organisationDescription: carry out conservation on a large scale, protect and restore habitats, and save species from extinction.

name: Quadram Institute Bioscience
organisationDescription: research how food and microbes interact, to promote health and prevent disease.

name: University of Newcastle upon Tyne
organisationDescription: a world-leading university, advancing knowledge, providing creative solutions, and addressing global problems.

name: VITO
organisationDescription: As a top European research centre, VITO and its 1,300 employees turn scientific insights into ground-breaking technological innovations, AI solutions and policy advice. We do so with a single objective: to help our world thrive for at least 1,000 more years.

name: Universite Toulouse
organisationDescription: The University of Toulouse, formerly Université Toulouse III - Paul Sabatier, is one of France's leading universities, with nearly 37,000 students. The diversity of its laboratories and the quality of its teaching in the fields of science, health, sport, technology, and engineering ensure its strong national and international scientific reputation. The university has 74 research laboratories and federative research structures. It is also home to a remarkable scientific heritage, including the Pic du Midi Observatory, a candidate for UNESCO World Heritage status.

name: Future Needs
organisationDescription: At Future Needs we look at things from the user perspective and we prioritize human factors in our studies and designs. We help our customers accomplish their development goals by addressing the future needs of their customers. We mainstream innovation by helping our clients turn research into products. We are a small team of senior consultants. In Future Needs we have grown professionally by working for respected employers all over Europe. Our next step to keep on growing was to become independent. And so we did.Our aim at Future Needs Management Consulting is to share our experience with small businesses and other types of innovative organisations to help them accomplish their R&D ambition.

name: SMHI
organisationDescription: SMHI is a Swedish expert authority with a global perspective and a vital task of predicting changes in weather, water and climate. Based on scientific foundations and through knowledge, research, and services, we contribute to increasing the sustainability of society as a whole. Every day, around the clock, throughout the year.

name: R-Cities
organisationDescription: For more than a decade, cities in our network have been shaping how urban resilience works in practice, developing new ways of governing, planning and investing, and demonstrating that resilience improves the everyday lives of people and communities. Chief Resilience Officers made this possible. Embedded inside local government, they connect departments, sustain momentum across political cycles and turn long-term priorities into action.

name: Meteo France
organisationDescription: As a public expert in weather and climate, Météo-France is at your side to contribute to your daily safety and help you make the best decisions in a changing climate.

name: NMA
organisationDescription: The National Meteorological Administration has as its main responsibility the protection of people's lives and material goods by transmitting weather forecasts and warnings, forecasts on the dispersion of atmospheric pollutants for situations of dangerous meteorological phenomena and accidental pollution, as well as agrometeorological forecasts to profile users. Specialized meteorological products are for a fee. They can contain diagnoses, current data from weather stations, forecasts, warnings and specialized weather bulletins, etc.

name: Universita Di Padova
organisationDescription: Silent witnesses of the long history of the University of Padua are also the palaces and monumental buildings that preserve centuries of knowledge and academic life. From Palazzo del Bo, the historic beating heart of the University, to the elegant frescoed rooms of Palazzo Cavalli, each building tells the story of the past of the University and of the city. Alongside these masterpieces of the past, today the University also looks to the future through urban regeneration and sustainable architecture projects . The twentieth-century buildings, created to meet the new needs of teaching and research, are flanked by new construction sites that redesign the university space with technologically advanced and environmentally friendly solutions. From the renovation of the Beato Pellegrino complex, intended for the humanities, to the most recent creations signed by internationally renowned architects, the University still proposes itself as a laboratory of ideas, experimentation and contemporary beauty.

name: Technical Unicercity of Denmark
organisationDescription: DTU is recognized internationally as a leading university in the areas of the technical and the natural sciences, renowned for our business-oriented approach, our focus on sustainability, and our amazing study environment.

name: CZU
organisationDescription: NO GOOD DESC

name: World Meteorological Organization
organisationDescription: WMO is the United Nations system's authoritative voice on the state and behaviour of the Earth's atmosphere, its interaction with the land and oceans, the weather and climate it produces and the resulting distribution of water resources. As weather, climate and the water cycle know no national boundaries, international cooperation at a global scale is essential for the development of meteorology and operational hydrology as well as to reap the benefits from their application. WMO provides the framework for such international cooperation for its 193 Member States and Territories. WMO's mandate relates to the areas of meteorology (weather and climate), operational hydrology and related geophysical sciences. WMO has a powerful role in contributing to the safety and welfare of humanity by fostering collaboration between its Members' National Meteorological and Hydrological Services (NMHSs) and advancing the application of meteorology and hydrology in many societal and economic areas.
